item.title: Hamas Operating Training Camps in Turkey for Attacks on Israel
item.description: Kan 11 News reports Hamas is using Turkish shooting ranges and drone schools to train operatives for future conflicts with Israel, raising tensions between Israel and Turkey.

item.title: North Korea to Deploy New Long-Range Artillery Targeting Seoul
item.description: North Korea announced it will deploy new long-range artillery systems capable of striking Seoul and commission its first destroyer, escalating military capabilities on the Korean Peninsula.

item.title: China to Supply J-35 Stealth Jets to Pakistan, Edge Over India
item.description: Chinese J-35 stealth fighter jets are set to give Pakistan an advantage over India in fifth-generation aircraft technology, as India lags in acquiring stealth capabilities.

item.title: UAE Air Defenses Respond to Missile Threat as Regional Tensions Escalate
item.description: UAE authorities activated air defenses in response to a missile threat amid ongoing US-Iran hostilities, urging residents to follow official channels for safety updates.

item.title: Third Round of Lebanon-Israel Direct Talks Set for Next Week in Washington
item.description: Lebanon and Israel will hold their third round of direct negotiations in Washington next week, mediated by the US State Department, continuing unprecedented diplomatic engagement.

item.title: US-Iran resolution at UN faces Chinese and Russian vetoes
item.description: The US urged countries to support a UN resolution demanding Iran halt attacks on the Strait of Hormuz, but diplomats say China and Russia are likely to veto it, highlighting geopolitical divisions over the conflict.

item.title: Pussy Riot protests Russia return to Venice Biennale
item.description: Dozens of activists from the anti-Kremlin art collective Pussy Riot demonstrated in Venice, waving Ukrainian flags and chanting against Putin to oppose Moscow presence at the Venice Biennale for the first time since the 2022 invasion.

item.title: Brazil Lula reports progress after Trump White House meeting
item.description: Brazilian President Lula reported progress in relations with the US after a three-hour meeting with Trump in Washington, seeking to avert new trade tariffs that had strained bilateral ties.

item.title: Serbia rejects local bid for Russian-owned NIS oil firm
item.description: Serbias President Vucic dismissed a bid by a recently formed local company to purchase a majority stake in the Russian-owned NIS oil firm, citing lack of expertise amid ongoing talks with Hungarian MOL.

item.title: Oil prices jump as US-Iran hostilities threaten ceasefire
item.description: Oil prices rose more than 1% after renewed fighting between the US and Iran threatened a shaky ceasefire and dashed hopes for progress on reopening the Strait of Hormuz, a key transit route.

item.title: US and Iran Exchange Fire in Strait of Hormuz, Testing Ceasefire
item.description: The US military said it carried out retaliatory strikes on Iran after intercepting Iranian attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, in the most serious test yet of their month-long ceasefire. Both sides said they did not want to escalate.

item.title: Trump Says Iran Peace Deal Could Be a Week Away
item.description: President Donald Trump said a peace deal with Iran could be reached within a week as Tehran reviews the latest US proposal. The US also paused its "Project Freedom" operations in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Russia Declares Ceasefire for WWII Anniversary as Ukraine Reports Violations
item.description: Russia declared a ceasefire from May 8-10 to mark the defeat of Nazi Germany, but President Zelenskiy said Russia continued to violate the Kyiv-proposed ceasefire. Over 50 drones were intercepted heading for Moscow.

item.title: Ukraine Peace Negotiator Arrives in Miami for Talks with US Envoys
item.description: Ukraine top negotiator Rustem Umerov arrived in Miami for meetings with US representatives as peace talks on ending the war with Russia have stalled in recent months.

item.title: Dollar Firms as US-Iran Hostilities Flare, Yen Steadies on Intervention Risk
item.description: The dollar strengthened against major currencies after renewed hostilities between the US and Iran, while the Japanese yen held steady following verbal intervention warnings from Tokyo.

item.title: UK Court Convicts Two Men for Spying on Hong Kong Dissidents
item.description: A UK court found two men guilty, including a British immigration officer, of conducting surveillance on Hong Kong pro-democracy activists in Britain on behalf of China, in an unprecedented shadow policing case.

item.title: Armenia Distances Itself from Russia as Kremlin Fumes Over Zelensky Visit
item.description: Armenian PM Pashinyan declared Armenia is not an ally of Russia on Ukraine, while Russia summoned Armenia's ambassador to protest Zelensky's visit to Yerevan, marking a significant shift in Caucasus geopolitics.

item.title: Hezbollah Claims 13 Attacks on Israeli Forces in Southern Lebanon
item.description: Hezbollah announced it carried out drone and artillery strikes on Israeli troops, Merkava tanks, an Iron Dome platform, and military positions in southern Lebanon amid ongoing cross-border escalation.

item.title: Turkey Unveils First Intercontinental Ballistic Missile Yildirimhan
item.description: Turkey revealed its first ICBM with a 6,000km range and Mach 25 speed, representing a major advancement in Turkish defense capabilities and regional deterrence.

item.title: US strikes Iranian military facilities after warship attacks in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US military targeted Iranian missile sites, command centers, and surveillance nodes after Iran fired on US Navy ships in the Strait of Hormuz. President Trump threatened further strikes if Iran does not accept a peace deal.

item.title: German finance minister blames Trump Iran war for economic slowdown
item.description: Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil called Trump's "irresponsible war in Iran" a key factor harming Germany's economy, highlighting growing transatlantic tensions over the conflict.

item.title: China announces suspended death sentences for former defense ministers
item.description: Two former Chinese defense ministers received suspended death sentences following a sweeping anti-corruption purge that has ousted several top military officials.

item.title: Fears of renewed Gaza war as Hamas disarmament talks stall
item.description: Israeli media reports suggest Israel is preparing to resume fighting amid an impasse in disarmament negotiations with Hamas, raising concerns about a new escalation.

item.title: Russia Victory Day parade held without tanks for first time in decades
item.description: Moscow's annual military parade will feature only soldiers, no military hardware, for the first time in nearly 20 years, seen as a sign that the Ukraine war is straining Russian military resources.

item.title: Saudi Arabia and Kuwait lift US military access restrictions
item.description: Saudi Arabia and Kuwait have lifted restrictions on US military use of their bases and airspace that were imposed after the launch of the US operation to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, according to the Wall Street Journal.
